source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
As shown in the figure, the angle between the smooth inclined rail $PA$ and the smooth horizontal rail $AB$ fixed on the ground is $\theta=53^{\circ}$, and the two rails are smoothly connected at point $A$. The rail $AB$ is tangent to the smooth semicircular track $BCD$ with radius $R{=}0.5\mathrm{m}$ at point $B$. $O$ is the center of the semicircle $BCD$, and $B$, $O$, $D$ lie on the same vertical line. To the right of $BD$, there exists a uniform electric field directed horizontally to the left, with a field strength of $E=6\times10^{6}\mathrm{V/m}$. An insulating small ball $a$ with mass $m{=}8\mathrm{g}$ is released from rest at a certain position on the inclined rail $PA$ and allowed to slide freely. Here, $g{=}10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$, $\sin53^{\circ}{=}0.8$, and $\cos53^{\circ}{=}0.6$.

1. If the small ball $a$ can just fly out from point $D$, find the height $h_{a}$ at which $a$ is released.
2. Let a ball $^b$ with mass $m{=}8\mathrm{g}$ and charge $q=+1\times10^{-8}\mathrm{C}$ be released from rest at a certain point on an inclined plane, and it just passes through point $C$ at the same height as the center of the circle. Find the height $h_{b}$ at which the ball $^b$ is released.
3. Under the condition of (2), if the length of the track $AB$ is $1.3\mathrm{m}$, and after passing point $D$, ball $b$ hits the track $PA$ vertically, find the height ${h_{b}}^{\prime}$ at which ball $b$ is released.

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'As shown in the figure'
the setup depends on geometric/spatial relations among rails, semicircular track, tangent point, center O, and vertical alignment of B/O/D
image asset exists in asset_registry_record.image_manifest and registry_passed=true
multi_step: the problem has three numbered sub-questions, each asking for a different release height under different physical conditions
solving requires combining mechanics on rails/semicircle with geometry and, in parts (2) and (3), electric-field effects
part (3) adds extra trajectory and perpendicular-impact constraints after leaving point D, indicating multi-condition reasoning
question_text is long and structurally dense, consistent with multi-stage derivation rather than single-step lookup
verifiability: answer_type is short_text and answer_text contains three explicit numeric results, one for each sub-question
the requested outputs are concrete heights with units/fractional forms, which are amenable to formula-based checking
physical givens are specific (m, q, E, R, g, trigonometric values), supporting deterministic verification
registry_passed=true and answer_present=true, though the multi-line open-text answer may require careful parsing

risk_flags: image-dependent geometry may be hard to verify if diagram interpretation is ambiguous
multi-part short-text answer may need alignment between three outputs and three sub-questions

decision_reasons: image-dependent geometry may be hard to verify if diagram interpretation is ambiguous
multi-part short-text answer may need alignment between three outputs and three sub-questions

normalization_record.normalized_question_text:
As shown in the figure, the angle between the smooth inclined rail $PA$ and the smooth horizontal rail $AB$ fixed on the ground is $\theta=53^{\circ}$, and the two rails are smoothly connected at point $A$. The rail $AB$ is tangent to the smooth semicircular track $BCD$ with radius $R{=}0.5\mathrm{m}$ at point $B$. $O$ is the center of the semicircle $BCD$, and $B$, $O$, $D$ lie on the same vertical line. To the right of $BD$, there exists a uniform electric field directed horizontally to the left, with a field strength of $E=6\times10^{6}\mathrm{V/m}$. An insulating small ball $a$ with mass $m{=}8\mathrm{g}$ is released from rest at a certain position on the inclined rail $PA$ and allowed to slide freely. Here, $g{=}10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$, $\sin53^{\circ}{=}0.8$, and $\cos53^{\circ}{=}0.6$.

1. If the small ball $a$ can just fly out from point $D$, find the height $h_{a}$ at which $a$ is released.

3. Under the condition of (2), if the length of the track $AB$ is $1.3\mathrm{m}$, and after passing point $D$, ball $b$ hits the track $PA$ vertically, find the height ${h_{b}}^{\prime}$ at which ball $b$ is released.

normalized_value.target_text: Under the condition of (2), if the length of the track $AB$ is $1.3\mathrm{m}$, and after passing point $D$, ball $b$ hits the track $PA$ vertically, find the height ${h_{b}}^{\prime}$ at which ball $b$ is released.

node_records.canonical_value:
As shown in the figure, the angle between the smooth inclined rail $PA$ and the smooth horizontal rail $AB$ fixed on the ground is $\theta=53^{\circ}$, and the two rails are smoothly connected at point $A$. The rail $AB$ is tangent to the smooth semicircular track $BCD$ with radius $R{=}0.5\mathrm{m}$ at point $B$. $O$ is the center of the semicircle $BCD$, and $B$, $O$, $D$ lie on the same vertical line. To the right of $BD$, there exists a uniform electric field directed horizontally to the left, with a field strength of $E=6\times10^{6}\mathrm{V/m}$. An insulating small ball $a$ with mass $m{=}8\mathrm{g}$ is released from rest at a certain position on the inclined rail $PA$ and allowed to slide freely. Here, $g{=}10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$, $\sin53^{\circ}{=}0.8$, and $\cos53^{\circ}{=}0.6$.

1. If the small ball $a$ can just fly out from point $D$, find the height $h_{a}$ at which $a$ is released.
2. Let a ball $^b$ with mass $m{=}8\mathrm{g}$ and charge $q=+1\times10^{-8}\mathrm{C}$ be released from rest at a certain point on an inclined plane, and it just passes through point $C$ at the same height as the center of the circle. Find the height $h_{b}$ at which the ball $^b$ is released.
3. Under the condition of (2), if the length of the track $AB$ is $1.3\mathrm{m}$, and after passing point $D$, ball $b$ hits the track $PA$ vertically, find the height ${h_{b}}^{\prime}$ at which ball $b$ is released.
surface_forms:
As shown in the figure, the angle between the smooth inclined rail $PA$ and the smooth horizontal rail $AB$ fixed on the ground is $\theta=53^{\circ}$, and the two rails are smoothly connected at point $A$. The rail $AB$ is tangent to the smooth semicircular track $BCD$ with radius $R{=}0.5\mathrm{m}$ at point $B$. $O$ is the center of the semicircle $BCD$, and $B$, $O$, $D$ lie on the same vertical line. To the right of $BD$, there exists a uniform electric field directed horizontally to the left, with a field strength of $E=6\times10^{6}\mathrm{V/m}$. An insulating small ball $a$ with mass $m{=}8\mathrm{g}$ is released from rest at a certain position on the inclined rail $PA$ and allowed to slide freely. Here, $g{=}10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$, $\sin53^{\circ}{=}0.8$, and $\cos53^{\circ}{=}0.6$.

1. If the small ball $a$ can just fly out from point $D$, find the height $h_{a}$ at which $a$ is released.
2. Let a ball $^b$ with mass $m{=}8\mathrm{g}$ and charge $q=+1\times10^{-8}\mathrm{C}$ be released from rest at a certain point on an inclined plane, and it just passes through point $C$ at the same height as the center of the circle. Find the height $h_{b}$ at which the ball $^b$ is released.
3. Under the condition of (2), if the length of the track $AB$ is $1.3\mathrm{m}$, and after passing point $D$, ball $b$ hits the track $PA$ vertically, find the height ${h_{b}}^{\prime}$ at which ball $b$ is released.

normalized_value.title: PhysReason 开放题
normalized_value.rewritten_question_text:
As shown in the figure, the angle between the smooth inclined rail $PA$ and the smooth horizontal rail $AB$ fixed on the ground is $\theta=53^{\circ}$, and the two rails are smoothly connected at point $A$. The rail $AB$ is tangent to the smooth semicircular track $BCD$ with radius $R{=}0.5\mathrm{m}$ at point $B$. $O$ is the center of the semicircle $BCD$, and $B$, $O$, $D$ lie on the same vertical line. To the right of $BD$, there exists a uniform electric field directed horizontally to the left, with a field strength of $E=6\times10^{6}\mathrm{V/m}$. An insulating small ball $a$ with mass $m{=}8\mathrm{g}$ is released from rest at a certain position on the inclined rail $PA$ and allowed to slide freely. Here, $g{=}10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$, $\sin53^{\circ}{=}0.8$, and $\cos53^{\circ}{=}0.6$.

1. If the small ball $a$ can just fly out from point $D$, find the height $h_{a}$ at which $a$ is released.
2. Let a ball $^b$ with mass $m{=}8\mathrm{g}$ and charge $q=+1\times10^{-8}\mathrm{C}$ be released from rest at a certain point on an inclined plane, and it just passes through point $C$ at the same height as the center of the circle. Find the height $h_{b}$ at which the ball $^b$ is released.
3. Under the condition of (2), if the length of the track $AB$ is $1.3\mathrm{m}$, and after passing point $D$, ball $b$ hits the track $PA$ vertically, find the height ${h_{b}}^{\prime}$ at which ball $b$ is released.

agent_assessment.rationale: The problem statement is complete and specifies all needed quantities and sub-questions. The diagram clearly shows the inclined rail, horizontal segment, semicircular track, labeled points, and leftward electric field region, so the text-image combination is semantically understandable for annotation.

cleaning_records.decision_rationale: The sample from PhysReason appears recoverable and should be reviewed rather than rejected.

conflict_pairs.detail: 文本中的视觉锚点明显多于可识别视觉区域。

The rail $AB$ is tangent to the smooth semicircular track $BCD$ with radius $R{=}0.5\mathrm{m}$ at point $B$. $O$ is the center of the semicircle $BCD$, and $B$, $O$, $D$ lie on the same vertical line. To the right of $BD$, there exists a uniform electric field directed horizontally to the left, with a field strength of $E=6\times10^{6}\mathrm{V/m}$. An insulating small ball $a$ with mass $m{=}8\mathrm{g}$ is released from rest at a certain position on the inclined rail $PA$ and allowed to slide freely.
